Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession number Phvul.007G056000 is listed in Table 1 and in Accession numbers.

Source_Species.reason: Common bean is the study species; synonymous with Phaseolus vulgaris throughout.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Phaseolus vulgaris is the experimental species named throughout the paper.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Methods state P. vulgaris cultivar Negro Jamapa was used and cloned sequences were obtained from this cultivar.

Applied_Species.reason: The gene was tested in common bean nodules.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species in the experiments is Phaseolus vulgaris.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods specify cultivar Negro Jamapa for P. vulgaris experiments.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: XMPP is explicitly called XMP phosphatase and recently demonstrated cytosolic enzyme in Arabidopsis, but the bean protein itself is not directly localized as cytosolic in Results.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Results conclude XMPP is involved in ureide biosynthesis via purine nucleotide catabolism.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Paper states XMPP dephosphorylates XMP to xanthosine and defines this as the XMP-xanthosine pathway.

Enzyme_Name.reason: XMPP is explicitly named xanthosine monophosphate phosphatase / XMP phosphatase.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number for XMPP appears in the paper.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Biochemical characterization and text state XMPP dephosphorylates XMP to xanthosine and phosphate.

Primary_Substrate.reason: XMP is the substrate assayed and discussed for XMPP.

Primary_Product.reason: Xanthosine is the reaction product stated in text.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: XMPP acts early in the pathway, before xanthosine/xanthine and before ureides.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: XMPP supplies xanthosine precursor for downstream allantoin/allantoate synthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper measures effects on allantoin and allantoate and identifies them as ureide end products.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Allantoin and allantoate are explicitly described as ureides.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Introduction states these ureides are used for long-distance nitrogen transport from nodules to shoot in tropical legumes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper states allantoate is high and locally focused in inner cortex/vascular region based partly on prior work and ALN promoter pattern, but does not directly show XMPP-specific terminal metabolite accumulation site in Results.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Table 1 gives XMPP nodule/root transcript ratio of 16.5 in P. vulgaris.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Promoter-reporter analyses showed pXMPP activity exclusively in infected cells.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The text refers to Arabidopsis XMPP as cytosolic and implies conservation, but bean XMPP subcellular localization was not directly tested in Results.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: xmpp nodules accumulated XMP and had reduced allantoate/allantoin, showing XMPP promotes ureide biosynthesis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are supported: biochemical validation, XMP accumulation in xmpp, and partial ureide reduction with bypass route.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Results include transient expression/enzyme assay, CRISPR knockout in hairy-root nodules, LC-MS metabolite profiling, and promoter-reporter analysis.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Methods specify nodules were formed after inoculation with Rhizobium tropici CIAT899.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: XMPP is clearly an entry enzyme and contributor, but the paper does not directly test or call it rate-limiting.

Omics_Data.reason: These SRA accessions are listed in Methods for transcriptome meta-analysis.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Engineering utility to enhance ureide production is not discussed in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Loss of XMPP reduced ureides, caused XMP accumulation, and increased flux to GMP/guanosine branch is explicitly discussed.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Km 7.3 ± 2.4 µM and kcat 18.4 ± 1.7 s−1 are reported; XMPP promoter activity in infected cells is shown, with later steps in uninfected cells discussed.

corrections.reason: No EC number for XMPP appears in the paper.

corrections.old_value: Useful target to enhance ureide production and nitrogen export, potentially in combination with downstream ureide-pathway genes; caution that loss can be partially bypassed via GMP-guanosine route
corrections.reason: Engineering utility to enhance ureide production is not discussed in the paper.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Phvul.009G220800 is listed for GSDA.2 in Table 1 and Accession numbers.

Source_Species.reason: Common bean is the source species.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Cultivar Negro Jamapa is specified in Methods.

Applied_Species.reason: GSDA.2 was tested in common bean nodules.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species is Phaseolus vulgaris.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: GSDA genes were investigated as part of ureide biosynthesis via purine catabolism.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The paper describes the alternative route from XMP to GMP via guanosine to xanthosine.

Enzyme_Name.reason: GSDA stands for guanosine deaminase in the paper.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number for GSDA.2 is provided.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper states guanosine is deaminated by GSDA to xanthosine.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: This is an upstream alternative branch feeding xanthosine before ureide formation.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: GSDA supplies xanthosine precursor for ureide synthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The branch is linked to allantoate/allantoin production in nodules.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: These metabolites serve long-distance nitrogen transport from nodules to shoot.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Mainly allantoate is the major product in nodules, but a precise 'mainly for export' site statement is not directly demonstrated for GSDA.2-specific experiments.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Table 1 shows GSDA.2 induced in nodules versus roots with ratio 2.8.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Promoter analyses showed GSDA.2 promoter active in infected cells.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: GSDA is described as cytosolic in Arabidopsis background, but bean GSDA.2 localization is not directly tested in Results.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Available GSDA mutants had less allantoate than control, showing contribution through the GMP-guanosine branch.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: GSDA.2 is the only slightly induced paralog, GSDA mutant combinations reduced allantoate, and GSDA promoters were active in infected cells.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: CRISPR knockout combinations, LC-MS profiling, and promoter-reporter analyses were used.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Nodules were formed with Rhizobium tropici CIAT899.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The value says not established, which matches the paper; no rate-limiting role is claimed there.

Omics_Data.reason: These RNA-seq datasets are listed in Methods.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Application for engineering is not proposed in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Discussion explicitly says this route is energetically more costly and may reflect surplus GMP turnover.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Editing of GSDA.1 failed; conclusions relied on gsda.2/gsda.3 and xmpp gsda.2 gsda.3 mutants; all three GSDA promoters were active in infected cells.

corrections.reason: No EC number for GSDA.2 is provided.

corrections.old_value: Can be used to support ureide production as a backup branch, especially when enhancing overall purine catabolic flux; less attractive alone because branch is energetically costly
corrections.reason: Application for engineering is not proposed in the paper.
